title: Decision quality report
summary: Use intent, claims, and evidence to decide whether the HTML report contract is ready to extend.

intent.primaryQuestion: Does the report give enough evidence to make the next implementation decision?
intent.decision: Proceed only if claims are traceable and unsupported claims are labeled honestly.
successCriteria: Assumptions and known limits are visible.
Actions are copyable without reading implementation details first.

claims.statement: Intent metadata should control density before visual modules are selected.
knownLimits: Intent is optional for backward compatibility.

claims.statement: A future release may add deeper automated accessibility tooling.
knownLimits: This change uses deterministic checks first.

sections.title: Reader actions
items: Check claim links before trusting the conclusion.
Run the fixture validator after generating the report.

verification.label: Claims linked
verification.detail: Each important claim names evidence or is marked as an assumption.

nextActions: Validate the generated HTML with validate-interaction.mjs.
